CONTENT.Early life: Kabha was born in Ein as-Sahla, Israel, to a Muslim-Arab family of Palestinian descent.
CONTENT.Club career: In summer 2015, after playing six seasons with Israeli side Maccabi Petah Tikva, he moved abroad and signed a three-year contract with Slovenian side NK Maribor.
CONTENT.International career: He was also part of the Israeli under-21 squad at the 2013 UEFA European Under-21 Championship.

SUMMARY: Marwan Kabha (Arabic: مروان كبها, Hebrew: מרואן קבהא; born 23 February 1991) is an Israeli professional footballer who plays a defensive midfielder for Liga Leumit club Maccabi Jaffa and the Israel national team.
